summary: Create canonical source-priority policy governing how Cici weights and trusts information from runtime memory, evidence, governed state, and external feeds.
rationale: The three-tier evidence model (Tier A = Xavier-verified, Tier B = structured summary with traceable source, Tier C = model synthesis / unverified recall) is referenced throughout CLAUDE.md and the BrewMind companion contract, but no governed artifact defines it. As a result, agent sessions and the proposal-reviewer must rely on prose docs rather than a machine-readable policy. Promoting this to a governed policy.json in source-priority/ completes the core governance layer and gives future agents a canonical source to validate against. This surface has status 'stub' in surface-map.json — this proposal fills it.
evidence_refs: docs/companion-agent/brewmind-companion-contract.md — Section B (Ephemeral vs Canonical) and Section C (Trust Tiers for Evidence)
decision_notes: Created users/cici/governed-state/source-priority/policy.json with four sections: source_hierarchy (5 ranked levels), evidence_tiers (A/B/C with definitions, examples, and permitted uses), conflict_resolution (4 rules + tension pattern), and agent_obligations (always/never lists). source-priority surface promoted from stub to active in surface-map.json.
